query: What are the torque specifications and warnings for the oil filter?
answer:
1. SAFETY:  
   - WARNING: HOT ENGINE COMPONENTS MAY CAUSE SKIN BURNS!  

2. ANSWER:  
   - The torque specifications for the oil filter are not explicitly mentioned in the provided context.  
   - Oil change intervals are specified as follows:  
     - Under normal operating conditions, oil must be changed every 100 hours.  
     - For AVGAS operation, oil should be changed every 50 hours.  
     - Under severe operating conditions, oil change frequency must be increased regardless of fuel type.  
   - Oil filter replacement should be done at least every 100 hours or once a year, whichever comes first.

# Technical Summary

This cluster documents the installation and maintenance procedures for the Garmin G500 integrated flight deck system on the Aquila AT01 A210 aircraft, covering avionics system integration across navigation, attitude, and collision warning functions. A maintenance technician would find detailed torque specifications, software version requirements (GDU 620 v3.01+, GRS 77 v2.12, GDC 74A v3.02), 12-month inspection intervals, and critical connector handling procedures—particularly for BNC connector alignment and pitot-static probe protection. query: Which tools are required for hydraulic system inspection?
answer:
1. SAFETY:  
   - [Warning|ATA-00] CAUT-00-0002: CLEANING AGENTS SHOULD NEVER BE LEFT IN ENGINE COMPARTMENT. CLEANER OR SOLVENT RESIDUE MAY CAUSE DAMAGE TO COMPONENTS SUCH AS NEOPRENE SEALS AND SILICONE FIRE SLEEVES.  
   - [Warning|ATA-00] NOTE-00-0001: USE COOLANT IN ACCORDANCE WITH MANUFACTURER'S INSTRUCTIONS.  
   - [Warning|ATA-00] CAUT-00-0001: CLEANING AGENTS SHOULD NEVER BE LEFT IN ENGINE COMPARTMENT. CLEANER OR SOLVENT RESIDUE MAY CAUSE DAMAGE TO COMPONENTS SUCH AS NEOPRENE SEALS AND SILICONE FIRE SLEEVES.  
   - [Warning|ATA-00] 5. CONTACT WITH HYDRAULIC FLUID CAN CAUSE SKIN IRRITATIONS. EXCESSIVE HYDRAULIC FLUID WILL ATTACK THE SURFACE OF VARIOUS MATERIALS. READ AND ADHERE TO ALL MANUFACTURERS INSTRUCTIONS. PROVIDE A SUITABLE COLLECTING VESSEL FOR HYDRAULIC FLUID.  

2. ANSWER:  
   The tools required for hydraulic system inspection include:  
   - A suitable collecting vessel for hydraulic fluid.  
   - A wrench or appropriate tool for disconnecting and connecting hydraulic fittings.  
   - A torque wrench to tighten bolts to the specified torque (10 - 12 Nm for wheel unit reassembly).  
   - A flashlight for visual inspection.  
   - A mirror for inspecting hard-to-see areas.  
   - Bleeder fittings for draining and refilling hydraulic fluid.  
   - Plugs or caps to prevent contaminants from entering hydraulic fittings, hoses, and lines.

A. The AQUILA AT01 main landing gear consists of the main landing gear struts, the wheels with brakes and the wheel fairings. The wheel fairings appertain for standard equipment. The main gear struts are spring legs. They made of high quality leaf steel and carry the landing loads. The AQUILA AT01 is equipped in series with disc type brakes. 2. B. Each main landing gear strut is attached to the fuselage structure by means of two aluminum mounting brackets. These brackets are bolted to the main landing gear ribs, which are bonded into the fuselage belly. To the inner bracket the struts are mounted with a special single bolt. The outer mounting bracket consists of two parts; the upper part locates on top of the main strut and the lower retaining bar locates below the main strut. The retaining bar is attached to the upper part of the bracket with two bolts. This arrangement ensures correct absorption of the landing loads like a rocker plate. To prevent chafing damage and allow angular movement of the struts, synthetic rubber inserts are installed between the contact surfaces. The wheel axle is attached to the lower end of the landing gear strut with four bolts. A mounting plate for the wheel fairing is installed on the inboard side of the lower end of each main landing gear strut using the bolts securing the wheel axle to strut. # Technical Summary

This cluster documents the **Lubrication System maintenance procedures** for the Aquila AT01 A210 aircraft, encompassing oil, hydraulic fluid, and grease application across engine, brake, and control systems. Technicians will find detailed guidance on lubricant specifications (4-stroke oil per ROTAX SI-912-016, MIL-H-5606 hydraulic fluid), service intervals (50-hour oil changes, bearing and brake lubrication schedules), and critical cleanliness protocols required for proper system operation. The cluster includes component locations (oil tank, filter assembly, engine cylinders, cooling systems), consumable types, hands-on procedural steps with specialized tools (hand grease dispensers), and essential warnings against chemical solvents and cable over-lubrication to prevent corrosion and component damage."]

query: List airworthiness requirements related to engine oil servicing.
answer:
1. SAFETY:  
   - [Warning|ATA-00] CAUT-00-0001: Cleaning agents should never be left in the engine compartment. Cleaner or solvent residue may cause damage to components such as neoprene seals and silicone fire sleeves.  
   - [Warning|ATA-71] CAUT-71-0002: Plug or cap all openings, hose, and lines immediately after disconnection to prevent entry of foreign material.  

2. ANSWER:  
   - Use only oil with API classification SF or SG (REQ-71-OIL_SPEC-0001).  
   - The engine oil capacity is 3.0 liters, with an initial filling of 3.5 liters and a minimum level of 2.0 liters (REQ-12-OIL-0001).  
   - Ensure the oil servicing area is clean and free of contaminants to avoid introducing foreign material into the engine system.  
   - Plug or cap all openings, hoses, and lines immediately after disconnection to prevent contamination (CAUT-71-0002).  
   - Avoid leaving cleaning agents or solvents in the engine compartment, as they may damage sensitive components (CAUT-00-0001).

# Technical Summary

This cluster documents the Power Plant System (engine and fuel delivery) for the Aquila AT01 A210, covering the Rotax 912S engine and its integrated subsystems including ignition, cooling, and engine controls. Technicians accessing this section will find component specifications, assembly procedures (exemplified by STEP-TASK-76-00-00-0201-001), and critical safety protocols such as throttle cable routing requirements to prevent fatigue failure. The inclusion of a winterization kit option and silicone sealant consumables indicates this documentation supports both standard operations and environmental adaptations. This represents a core engine maintenance and inspection module essential for pre-flight checks and scheduled servicing."]

query: What maintenance steps should be followed before fuel system work?
answer:
1. **SAFETY**  
   - [Warning|ATA-28] **WARN-28-0001**: Accuracy of the indicating system strongly depends on the fuel grade used for calibration. Refer to SB-AT01-020 for further information. System calibration should be performed with unleaded super (RON 95) or super plus (RON 98) fuel grade. Do not use mixtures of fuel grade for calibration.  
   - [Warning|ATA-28] **WARN-28-0002**: For U.S. registered aircraft and aircraft registered where the FAA TC has been accepted, system calibration must be performed with AVGAS 100LL or AVGAS UL91 fuel grade. Do not use unleaded super (RON 95) or super plus (RON 98) or mixtures of fuel grade for calibration.  
   - [Warning|ATA-28] **WARN-28-0003**: Fuel gauge calibrated with AVGAS. Indicated fuel level may differ from actual fuel level if MOGAS is used.  

2. **ANSWER**  
   - Ensure electrical power to the aircraft is OFF.  
   - Disconnect the battery (refer to 24-30-00).  
   - Drain fuel from the wing fuel tanks.  
   - Verify the fuel selector/shut-off valve is in the OFF position.  
   - Level the aircraft laterally and longitudinally (refer to 08-10-00).  
   - Open the access plate 610 BB / 510 BB (refer to 06-30-00).  
   - Disconnect the fuel outlet line and fuel return line from the fuel tank.  
   - Cap the fuel tank vent line.  
   - Plug or cap all openings, hoses, and lines immediately after disconnection to prevent entry of foreign material.

# Technical Summary

This cluster documents the landing gear and brake system maintenance procedures for the Aquila AT01 A210, encompassing inspections, component replacements, and operational checks across nose and main gear assemblies plus associated hydraulic brake components. Technicians will find detailed requirements for critical maintenance tasks including brake disc scoring limits (≤0.5 mm), lining thickness minimums (≥2.5 mm), strut overhauls at 6000-hour intervals, and mandatory inspections following hard landings or damage incidents. The documentation integrates procedural steps, specialized tools (borescope, tap test hammer, fluid pump), consumable specifications (hydraulic fluid), and system-level interdependencies between the landing gear, brakes, and lighting systems, with particular emphasis on safety-critical operations such as brake bleeding and wheel deflation protocols."]

query: What are the torque specifications and warnings for the oil filter?
answer:
1. **SAFETY**  
   - **CAUTION**: Under severe operating conditions, the frequency of oil changes must be increased regardless of the type of fuel used (MOGAS or AVGAS).  
   - **WARNING**: HOT ENGINE COMPONENTS MAY CAUSE SKIN BURNS!  

2. **ANSWER**  
   - The torque specification for the oil filter is not explicitly mentioned in the provided context. However, the oil filter should be tightened by hand until snug.  
   - The oil filter should be replaced at least every 50 hours for engines operating with AVGAS or every 100 hours under normal operating conditions.  
   - When replacing the oil filter, lubricate the mating sealing ring of the new oil filter with engine oil before installation.  

3. **SOURCE**

query: Which tools are required for hydraulic system inspection?
answer:
1. SAFETY:  
   - Contact with hydraulic fluid can cause skin irritations. Excessive hydraulic fluid will attack the surface of various materials. Read and adhere to all manufacturers' instructions. Provide a suitable collecting vessel for hydraulic fluid.

2. ANSWER:  
   - The tools required for hydraulic system inspection are not explicitly listed in the provided context. However, based on general maintenance practices for hydraulic systems, typical tools may include:  
     - Wrenches or socket sets for disconnecting and connecting hydraulic fittings.  
     - Bleeder fittings for draining and refilling hydraulic fluid.  
     - Flashlight for visual inspection.  
     - Torque wrench for tightening bolts to specified torque values.  
     - Plugs or caps to prevent the entry of contaminants into open hydraulic lines.  
     - Collection vessel for draining hydraulic fluid.  

   For specific tools required for the Aquila AT01, refer to the detailed maintenance manual or contact the manufacturer.

3. SOURCE: Graph Context

A. The AQUILA AT01 main landing gear consists of the main landing gear struts, the wheels with brakes and the wheel fairings. The wheel fairings appertain for standard equipment. The main gear struts are spring legs. They made of high quality leaf steel and carry the landing loads. The AQUILA AT01 is equipped in series with disc type brakes. 2. B. Each main landing gear strut is attached to the fuselage structure by means of two aluminum mounting brackets. These brackets are bolted to the main landing gear ribs, which are bonded into the fuselage belly. To the inner bracket the struts are mounted with a special single bolt. The outer mounting bracket consists of two parts; the upper part locates on top of the main strut and the lower retaining bar locates below the main strut. The retaining bar is attached to the upper part of the bracket with two bolts. This arrangement ensures correct absorption of the landing loads like a rocker plate. To prevent chafing damage and allow angular movement of the struts, synthetic rubber inserts are installed between the contact surfaces. The wheel axle is attached to the lower end of the landing gear strut with four bolts. A mounting plate for the wheel fairing is installed on the inboard side of the lower end of each main landing gear strut using the bolts securing the wheel axle to strut. query: List airworthiness requirements related to engine oil servicing.
answer:
1. **SAFETY**:  
   - Always ensure the aircraft is parked on a level surface and the engine is off before servicing the oil system.  
   - Use proper personal protective equipment (PPE) when handling oil to avoid skin or eye contact.  
   - Avoid overfilling the oil tank to prevent overflow and potential fire hazards.  

2. **ANSWER**:  
   - **Oil Type and Specifications**: Use only oil conforming to the manufacturer's specifications as outlined in the maintenance manual.  
   - **Oil Level Checks**: Regularly check the oil level using the sight glass or dipstick, ensuring it is within the recommended range.  
   - **Oil Drain and Refill**: When draining or refilling oil, ensure the aircraft is on a level surface and the oil is at operating temperature for accurate measurement.  
   - **Oil Filter and Strainer Maintenance**: Inspect and clean the oil suction strainer and oil filter regularly. Replace if metal particles or excessive debris are found.  
   - **Oil Cooler Maintenance**: Check the oil cooler for deformation, obstruction, or damage. Clean as necessary to ensure proper cooling.  
   - **Oil Temperature and Pressure Monitoring**: Ensure the oil temperature and pressure measuring systems are functioning correctly. Replace faulty sensors or gauges.  
   - **Oil Consumption Monitoring**: Track oil consumption between services. Excessive consumption may indicate engine wear or failure.

query: What maintenance steps should be followed before fuel system work?
answer:
1. **SAFETY**  
   - **WARNING**: SERVICEABLE FIRE FIGHTING EQUIPMENT MUST BE AVAILABLE.  
   - **CAUTION**: AIRCRAFT AND FILLING FITTINGS MUST BE GROUNDED. ALL ELECTRICAL EQUIPMENT IN THE AIRCRAFT SHOULD BE TURNED OFF. THE ALT / BAT SWITCH SHOULD BE IN THE OFF POSITION AND THE IGNITION KEY REMOVED FROM THE AIRCRAFT. NO SMOKING!  
   - **CAUTION**: NEVER APPLY REGULATED OR UNREGULATED AIR FROM AN AIR COMPRESSOR TO THE FUEL SYSTEM OR COMPONENTS.  
   - **CAUTION**: DO NOT PRESSURIZE THE FUEL SYSTEM TO MORE THAN 1.0 PSI. DAMAGE MAY OCCUR TO SYSTEM COMPONENTS IF MORE THAN 1.0 PSI IS APPLIED.  

2. **ANSWER**  
   - Ensure serviceable fire fighting equipment is available and within easy access.  
   - Ground the aircraft and filling fittings.  
   - Turn off all electrical equipment in the aircraft.  
   - Set the ALT / BAT switch to the OFF position and remove the ignition key.  
   - Avoid wearing clothing that generates static electricity (e.g., synthetic fabrics).  
   - Perform fuel system servicing only in a designated fuel loading/unloading area.  
   - Turn off equipment near the aircraft.  
   - Do not operate any electrical devices during fueling.

Safety Precautions SERVICEABLE FIRE FIGHTING EQUIPMENT MUST BE AVAILABLE . AIRCRAFT AND FILLING FITTINGS MUST BE GROUNDED. ALL ELECTRICAL EQUIPMENT IN THE AIRCRAFT SHOULD BE TURNED OFF. THE ALT / BAT SWITCH SHOULD BE IN THE OFF POSITION AND THE IGNITION KEY REMOVED FROM THE AIRCRAFT. NO SMOKING! (1) Before beginning maintenance, a serviceable fire extinguisher (at least foam extinguisher) must be positioned within easy access. (2) Do not wear clothing that has a tendency to generate static electricity (i.e. synthetic fabrics). (3) No metal tabs on footwear. (4) Carry out fuel system servicing procedures only in a designated fuel loading/unloading area. (5) Ground equipment near the aircraft must be turned off. (6) While filling do not turn on any electrical device. (7) Make sure that the aircraft and filling fittings are properly grounded: (a) First ground the aircraft; (b) If a mobile filling device is being used, ground the filling device (same potential as aircraft); (c) Ground the mobile filling device with the aircraft. General NOTE: Safety and Maintenance Precautions WARNING: DURING ALL FUEL SYSTEM SERVICING PROCEDURES " " Servicing 30.10.13 MAINTENANCE MANUAL AQUILA AT01 A 12-11-00 Page 302 B. Maintenance Precautions (1) Use designated equipment for fuel loading / unloading to prevent contamination. (2) Only use approved anti-icing additive. (3) Blend fuel in accordance with prescribed procedures. (4) Document all fuel blending. 3. A. Fueling (1) Move aircraft to a designated fuel loading / fuel unloading area. (2) Make sure that the ALT / BAT switch is in the OFF position. (3) Ground aircraft and filling device as described above. (4) Position a fire extinguisher near to the fuel tank to be serviced. (5) Remove fuel filler cap and fill fuel tank to desired level. (6) Remove fuel service nozzle and install fuel cap. (7) Move fire extinguisher and the fuel service nozzle to the other tank to be filled. (10) Check correct lock of both fuel filler caps. Remove excess fuel from the wing area using a cloth. (11) Remove ground cables. (12) Compare reading of fueled amount on filling device with readings on the fuel indicators in the aircraft. B. Defueling (1) Move aircraft to a designated fuel loading / fuel unloading area. (2) Make sure you have enough fuel collectors. (3) Make sure that the ALT / BAT switch is in OFF position. (4) Ground aircraft and filling device as described above. (5) (6) Remove fuel cap and remove as much fuel as possible using a defueling nozzle. (8) (11) Drain remaining fuel from each wing fuel tank. (12) Drain remaining fuel from the drain valve located at the base of the electrical fuel pump with the fuel selector valve in the position LEFT and then in the position RIGHT. (13) Make sure all drain valves are closed"]
